stat_of_week: .636 - James Wood posted a .636 OBP for the week, the highest single-player OBP mark seen this season and one of the most dominant offensive performances in recent memory
thriller_note: A dead-even tie where General Hospital's massive pitching workload (70 K, 69 IP) offset Buttville's strong ERA (2.25), leaving both teams to split points in a matchup that neither could close out, keeping both in the middle of a crowded standings chase.
key_storyline: The Athletic Park Dragons erupted for the most dominant offensive performance of the week, led by James Wood's historic .636 OBP and Willson Contreras's 11 RBI, propelling them to a 6–3 win over the Wisconsin Rapids Reapers and vaulting them back into the upper tier of the league. Meanwhile, the Reapers — despite posting a sparkling 1.65 ERA and 1.04 WHIP — were simply outgunned on the offensive side, winning only the pitching categories they could control. The contrast illustrated perfectly how this league's dual-category format can punish even elite pitching staffs when the bats go cold.
lucky_reason: The Benchwarmers posted the league's highest luck delta (+0.91) despite a weak pitching line (42.1 IP, 5.53 ERA, only 34 K) and below-average hitting totals, winning categories they had little business winning given their overall roster output this week.
unlucky_reason: The Wonderdogs dominated pitching with a 4.30 ERA, 1.13 WHIP, 62 K, and 7 NSVH in 67 innings — genuinely elite pitching numbers — yet finished with the league's worst luck delta (-0.60) and barely squeaked out a 5–4 win despite deserving a more comfortable margin based on their category profile.
records_broken: North Correa's 11 stolen bases this week ties their own league record for most SB in a single week (set in week 3, the current week), which stands as the all-time single-week SB mark in this league.
